title: Mustard-Gooey Filipino Leche Flan
description: A unique twist on traditional Filipino leche flan, using white bread mix and a hint of mustard for added depth.

ingredients.name: White Bread Mix
ingredients.notes: Softened at room temperature for 30 minutes.

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C) and arrange a baking dish with 6 ramekins.
In a blender or food processor, combine the softened white bread mix, granulated sugar, egg yolks, whole milk, mustard, and salt. Blend until smooth.
Strain the mixture into a large bowl to remove any bread lumps.
Add the water, unsalted butter, bonito shavings, and vanilla extract to the bowl. Mix until well combined.
Pour the mixture into the prepared ramekins, filling them about 3/4 of the way full.
Place the ramekins in the baking dish and add hot water to come halfway up the sides of the ramekins.
Bake for 45-50 minutes or until the edges are set and the centers are still slightly jiggly.
Remove the ramekins from the water bath and let them cool to room temperature.
Chill in the refrigerator for at least 4 hours or overnight.
To serve, run a knife around the edges of each ramekin and invert onto plates.
